title: Public A733 datasheet security-fabric audit
question: Does the public A733 datasheet expose a patchable SDMMC0 security/fabric control clue for IDMAC descriptor access?

findings.evidence: Datasheet says A733 has four SMHC interfaces; SMHC0 is SD3.0, SMHC1 is SDIO3.0, SMHC2/3 are eMMC5.1.
findings.impact: Confirms SDMMC0 role but provides no IDMAC descriptor-fetch register, compatible difference, or bus-master setup.

findings.evidence: Datasheet says SPC sets secure DRAM and peripheral areas, and TZMA sets secure SRAM areas; both are secure-CPU-only.
findings.impact: Supports the broad security-fabric suspicion, but gives no offsets, bitfields, SMHC mapping, Linux API, or safe non-secure patch path.

findings.evidence: Web searches for A733/SUN60IW2 TRM plus SPC/TZMA/SMHC did not find a register-level manual; local source grep found no A733 SPC/TZMA driver or SDMMC property.
findings.impact: No source-backed behavior patch can be made from the datasheet alone.

decision: Do not patch. Treat SPC as a named class to ask maintainers/vendor about, not as an implementable kernel delta until register-level source or firmware handoff details appear.
next_responsible_step: If a reply mentions SPC/TZMA or an address range, build a read-only diagnostic first; otherwise continue waiting/source acquisition.
